name: Atomic Nucleus

phase_0.boundary_condition: The finite range of the strong nuclear potential well that confines quarks and nucleons against electromagnetic repulsion.

VINIT.justification: Represents the initial void state of free quarks and gluons prior to color confinement and hadronization.

TANCH.justification: Defines the closed spatial limit of the strong force where nucleons are confined and the system terminates.

AFWD.justification: The directed forward morphism of assembling free nucleons into a heavier, higher-mass compound nucleus.

AREV.justification: The reverse descent transformation where a neutron decays into a proton, electron, and antineutrino via the weak force.

CLINK.justification: The sequential chaining of color charge interactions that binds quarks into nucleons and nucleons into nuclei.

IMSCRIB.justification: The self-referential identity of the nucleon that remains invariant and recognizes itself through all strong force interactions.

FSPLIT.justification: Branches a heavy parent nucleus into two distinct paths: a lighter daughter nucleus and an emitted alpha particle.

FFUSE.justification: Reconstitutes the original parent nucleus by fusing the daughter nucleus and the alpha particle back together.

EVALT.justification: The affirmative evaluation where the daughter nucleus remains intact in a lower energy bound state.

EVALF.justification: The negative evaluation where the alpha particle escapes the potential well by quantum tunneling through the Coulomb barrier.

ENGAGR.justification: A paradice state where the nucleus simultaneously holds both an excited metastable spin configuration and the ground state without resolution.

IFIX.justification: The permanent append-only record of the atomic number and mass number that irreversibly defines the specific isotope.

phase_3.void_description: The unconfined state of free quarks and gluons in the vacuum prior to color confinement and nucleon formation.
phase_3.true_description: A stable bound configuration of nucleons residing entirely within the attractive strong nuclear potential well.
phase_3.false_description: An unbound dissociated state where nucleons have overcome the strong force barrier and separated.
phase_3.both_description: A quantum tunneling resonance where the alpha particle exists simultaneously inside and outside the Coulomb barrier.

steps.domain_action: Initialize the unconfined vacuum state of free quarks and gluons prior to hadronization.
Bind quarks via gluon exchange to form discrete protons and neutrons.
Establish the self-identity of the nucleon, conserving baryon number through strong interactions.
Assemble nucleons into a heavier compound nucleus via the attractive strong nuclear force.
Confine the assembled nucleons within the finite boundary of the nuclear potential well.
Branch the unstable parent nucleus into a daughter nucleus and an emitted alpha particle.
Anchor the T-arm as the daughter nucleus remains in a bound lower-energy state.
Anchor the F-arm as the alpha particle tunnels through the Coulomb barrier to escape.
Recombine the daughter nucleus and alpha particle to recover the original parent nucleus.
Execute beta decay, transforming a neutron into a proton via the weak force descent.
Enter a metastable nuclear isomer state, holding excited and ground spin configurations simultaneously.
Permanently record the atomic number and mass number to fix the final isotope identity.
Chain residual strong force interactions to maintain the cohesion of the final nucleus.
Re-verify the conserved quantum numbers of the final nuclear state.
Seal the system at the absolute boundary of the strong force interaction range.

phase_5.compiler_frontend: Quantum chromodynamics and the electroweak theory governing nucleon interactions and decay pathways.
phase_5.ipc_mechanism: Exchange of virtual mesons and gluons mediating the strong and residual strong forces between nucleons.
phase_5.memory_mechanism: The quantized energy levels and spin states of the nucleons within the nuclear shell model.
phase_5.scheduler_mechanism: The probabilistic decay constants and half-lives dictating the timing of radioactive transitions.
phase_5.alfs_store: The chart of nuclides mapping all known isotopes and their fundamental nuclear properties.

phase_6.cycle_cost: Mass-energy equivalence released or absorbed as binding energy during nucleon rearrangement.
phase_6.pre_cycle_state: A bound or unbound configuration of nucleons at a specific initial energy state.
phase_6.post_cycle_state: A reconfigured nuclear state with a different binding energy and emitted or absorbed particles.
phase_6.delta_s_verdict: ΔS ≈ 0 — The strong force interactions are time-reversible and conserve total energy and baryon number, maintaining thermodynamic equilibrium at the quantum level.

lean_scaffold:
-- IGProtocol scaffold: VINIT → CLINK → IMSCRIB → AFWD → TANCH → FSPLIT → EVALT → EVALF → FFUSE → AREV → ENGAGR → IFIX → CLINK → IMSCRIB → TANCH
-- Class: Atomic Nucleus
-- Fingerprint: sig=(9,2,3,1)
--   self_ref=False | frobenius_order=1
--   dialetheia_complete=True | period=15
-- Expected tier: O₂
-- FSPLIT/FFUSE pairs: [(5, 8)]

import Imscribing.IGMorphism
import Imscribing.IGFunctor

namespace Imscribing
open Primitives Frobenius IGProtocol
open Dimensionality Topology Relational Polarity Grammar
     Fidelity KineticChar Granularity Criticality Protection Stoichiometry Chirality

-- ── Token → IG field mapping ──────────────────────────────────────────────
--   [0] VINIT     dim    := 𐑼               𐑼 → 𐑱  | initial object — ground of distinction
--   [1] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑼 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [2] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑾  | identity — self-imscription
--   [3] AFWD      rel    := 𐑾               𐑠 → 𐑡  | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow
--   [4] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑾 → 𐑚  | terminal object — connectivity boundary
--   [5] FSPLIT    gran   := 𐑚               𐑚 → 𐑚  | split δ — range decomposition
--   [6] EVALT     crit   := ⊙               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-true — criticality gate open
--   [7] EVALF     chir   := 𐑖               𐑚 → 𐑙  | evaluate-false — chirality check
--   [8] FFUSE     stoi   := 𐑙               𐑙 → 𐑗  | fuse μ — assembly mode
--   [9] AREV      pol    := 𐑗               𐑙 → 𐑳  | reverse morphism — parity flip
--   [10] ENGAGR    stoi   := 𐑳               𐑗 → 𐑭  | engage paradox — B-state, both arms
--   [11] IFIX      prot   := 𐑭               𐑳 → 𐑱  | irreversible fixation — winding number
--   [12] CLINK     fid    := 𐑱               𐑭 → 𐑠  | composition — regime coherence
--   [13] IMSCRIB   gram   := 𐑠               𐑱 → 𐑡  | identity — self-imscription
--   [14] TANCH     top    := 𐑡               𐑠 → 𐑼  | terminal object — connectivity boundary

-- ── Main IGProtocol term ────────────────────────────────────────────────────

noncomputable def atomic_nucleus_protocol : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  .withGram 𐑠 <|
  -- Seq chain:
  (.arrow 𐑼 𐑼 𐑱)  -- [0] VINIT | dim := 𐑼 | initial object — ground of distinction (Initialize the unconfined vacuum state of free quarks and gluons prior to had...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑼 𐑠)  -- [1] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Bind quarks via gluon exchange to form discrete protons and neutrons.)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑾)  -- [2] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Establish the self-identity of the nucleon, conserving baryon number through ...)
  (.arrow 𐑾 𐑠 𐑡)  -- [3] AFWD | rel := 𐑾 | forward morphism — bidirectional arrow (Assemble nucleons into a heavier compound nucleus via the attractive strong n...)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑾 𐑚)  -- [4] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Confine the assembled nucleons within the finite boundary of the nuclear pote...)
  -- FSPLIT [5] (gran := 𐑚) (Branch the unstable parent nucleus into a daughter nucleus and an emitted alpha particle.) / FFUSE [8] (stoi := 𐑙)
  .seq
    (.prod
      -- T-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow ⊙ 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [6] EVALT | crit := ⊙ | evaluate-true — criticality gate open (Anchor the T-arm as the daughter nucleus remains in a bound lower-energy state.)
      -- F-branch (1 nodes)
      (.arrow 𐑖 𐑚 𐑙)  -- [7] EVALF | chir := 𐑖 | evaluate-false — chirality check (Anchor the F-arm as the alpha particle tunnels through the Coulomb barrier to...))
    -- reconnect at FFUSE [8]: μ closes the Frobenius pair
    (.arrow 𐑙 𐑙 𐑗)  -- [8] FFUSE | stoi := 𐑙 (Recombine the daughter nucleus and alpha particle to recover the original parent nucleus.)
  (.arrow 𐑗 𐑙 𐑳)  -- [9] AREV | pol := 𐑗 | reverse morphism — parity flip (Execute beta decay, transforming a neutron into a proton via the weak force d...)
  (.arrow 𐑳 𐑗 𐑭)  -- [10] ENGAGR | stoi := 𐑳 | engage paradox — B-state, both arms (Enter a metastable nuclear isomer state, holding excited and ground spin conf...)
  (.arrow 𐑭 𐑳 𐑱)  -- [11] IFIX | prot := 𐑭 | irreversible fixation — winding number (Permanently record the atomic number and mass number to fix the final isotope...)
  (.arrow 𐑱 𐑭 𐑠)  -- [12] CLINK | fid := 𐑱 | composition — regime coherence (Chain residual strong force interactions to maintain the cohesion of the fina...)
  (.arrow 𐑠 𐑱 𐑡)  -- [13] IMSCRIB | gram := 𐑠 | identity — self-imscription (Re-verify the conserved quantum numbers of the final nuclear state.)
  (.arrow 𐑡 𐑠 𐑼)  -- [14] TANCH | top := 𐑡 | terminal object — connectivity boundary (Seal the system at the absolute boundary of the strong force interaction range.)

-- ── Evaluation arm sub-defs ─────────────────────────────────────────────────

-- truth arm
noncomputable def atomic_nucleus_true_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (atomic_nucleus_protocol).restrictToEVALT

-- false arm
noncomputable def atomic_nucleus_false_arm : IGProtocol 𐑼 𐑡 :=
  (atomic_nucleus_protocol).restrictToEVALF

-- ── Verification theorems ───────────────────────────────────────────────────

theorem atomic_nucleus_tier : TierFunctor.obj 𐑼 = .O₂ := by decide

-- Frobenius (split → fuse): μ∘δ = id on .prod branch
-- Proof: apply igFrobAlg_self_fusion; exact mu_delta_A_id
-- (requires mu_delta_A_id from IGFunctor library)

end Imscribing
